sampleDataDialog.createPrompt: Create a container "{{containerName}}" and import sample data into it. This may take a few minutes.
sampleDataDialog.errorContainerExists: The container "{{containerName}}" in database "{{databaseName}}" already exists. Please delete it and retry.

documents.unsavedChangesMessage: Your unsaved changes will be lost. Do you want to continue?
documents.throttlingError: Some documents failed to delete due to a rate limiting error. Please try again later. To prevent this in the future, consider increasing the throughput on your container or database.
documents.missingShardProperty: The document is lacking the shard property: {{partitionKeyProperty}}
documents.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {{documentName}}?
documents.deleteDocumentFailedLog: Failed to delete document {{documentId}} with status code {{statusCode}}
documents.deleteThrottledLog: Failed to delete {{count}} document(s) due to "Request too large" (429) error. Retrying...
documents.missingShardKeyLog: Failed to save new document: Document shard key not defined
documents.filterTooltip: Type a query predicate or choose one from the list.
documents.requestTooLargeBase: Some delete requests failed due to a "Request too large" exception (429)
documents.increaseThroughputTip: To prevent this in the future, consider increasing the throughput on your container or database.
documents.mongoFilterPlaceholder: Type a query predicate (e.g., {"id":"foo"}), or choose one from the drop down list, or leave empty to query all documents.
documents.sqlFilterPlaceholder: Type a query predicate (e.g., WHERE c.id="1"), or choose one from the drop down list, or leave empty to query all documents.

storedProcedure.idPlaceholder: Enter the new stored procedure id

udf.idPlaceholder: Enter the new user defined function id

conflicts.changesWillBeLost: Changes will be lost. Do you want to continue?

deleteDatabase.warningMessage: Warning! The action you are about to take cannot be undone. Continuing will permanently delete this resource and all of its children resources.
deleteDatabase.inputMismatch: Input {{databaseName}} name "{{input}}" does not match the selected {{databaseName}} "{{selectedId}}"
deleteDatabase.feedbackTitle: Help us improve Azure Cosmos DB!
deleteDatabase.feedbackReason: What is the reason why you are deleting this {{databaseName}}?

deleteCollection.inputMismatch: Input id {{input}} does not match the selected {{selectedId}}
deleteCollection.feedbackTitle: Help us improve Azure Cosmos DB!
deleteCollection.feedbackReason: What is the reason why you are deleting this {{collectionName}}?

addDatabase.databaseTooltip: A {{databaseLabel}} is a logical container of one or more {{collectionsLabel}}
addDatabase.shareThroughputTooltip: Provisioned throughput at the {{databaseLabel}} level will be shared across all {{collectionsLabel}} within the {{databaseLabel}}.
addDatabase.greaterThanError: Please enter a value greater than {{minValue}} for autopilot throughput
addDatabase.acknowledgeSpendErrorMonthly: Please acknowledge the estimated monthly spend.
addDatabase.acknowledgeSpendErrorDaily: Please acknowledge the estimated daily spend.

addCollection.databaseTooltip: A database is analogous to a namespace. It is the unit of management for a set of {{collectionName}}.
addCollection.shareThroughputTooltip: Throughput configured at the database level will be shared across all {{collectionName}} within the database.
addCollection.collectionIdTooltip: Unique identifier for the {{collectionName}} and used for id-based routing through REST and all SDKs.
addCollection.shardingTooltip: Sharded collections split your data across many replica sets (shards) to achieve unlimited scalability. Sharded collections require choosing a shard key (field) to evenly distribute your data.
addCollection.hierarchicalPartitionKeyInfo: This feature allows you to partition your data with up to three levels of keys for better data distribution. Requires .NET V3, Java V4 SDK, or preview JavaScript V3 SDK.
addCollection.provisionDedicatedThroughputTooltip: You can optionally provision dedicated throughput for a {{collectionName}} within a database that has throughput provisioned. This dedicated throughput amount will not be shared with other {{collectionNamePlural}} in the database and does not count towards the throughput you provisioned for the database. This throughput amount will be billed in addition to the throughput amount you provisioned at the database level.
addCollection.analyticalStoreSynapseLinkRequired: Azure Synapse Link is required for creating an analytical store {{collectionName}}. Enable Synapse Link for this Cosmos DB account.
addCollection.mongoIndexingTooltip: The _id field is indexed by default. Creating a wildcard index for all fields will optimize queries and is recommended for development.
addCollection.createWildcardIndex: Create a Wildcard Index on all fields
addCollection.legacySdkCheckbox: My application uses an older Cosmos .NET or Java SDK version (.NET V1 or Java V2)
addCollection.legacySdkInfo: To ensure compatibility with older SDKs, the created container will use a legacy partitioning scheme that supports partition key values of size only up to 101 bytes. If this is enabled, you will not be able to use hierarchical partition keys.
addCollection.indexingOnInfo: All properties in your documents will be indexed by default for flexible and efficient queries.
addCollection.indexingOffInfo: Indexing will be turned off. Recommended if you don't need to run queries or only have key value operations.
addCollection.indexingOffWarning: By creating this container with indexing turned off, you will not be able to make any indexing policy changes. Indexing changes are only allowed on a container with a indexing policy.
addCollection.acknowledgeSpendErrorMonthly: Please acknowledge the estimated monthly spend.
addCollection.acknowledgeSpendErrorDaily: Please acknowledge the estimated daily spend.
addCollection.unshardedMaxRuError: Unsharded collections support up to 10,000 RUs
addCollection.acknowledgeShareThroughputError: Please acknowledge the estimated cost of this dedicated throughput.
addCollection.vectorPolicyError: Please fix errors in container vector policy
addCollection.fullTextSearchPolicyError: Please fix errors in container full text search policy
addCollection.newDatabaseIdAriaLabel: New database id, Type a new database id

teachingBubble.step1Body: Database is the parent of a container. You can create a new database or use an existing one. In this tutorial we are creating a new database named SampleDB.
teachingBubble.step2Body: Cosmos DB recommends sharing throughput across database. Autoscale will give you a flexible amount of throughput based on the max RU/s set (Request Units).
teachingBubble.step4Body: Last step - you will need to define a partition key for your collection. /address was chosen for this particular example. A good partition key should have a wide range of possible value
teachingBubble.step5Body: A sample container is now being created and we are adding sample data for you. It should take about 1 minute.
teachingBubble.step5BodyFollowUp: Once the sample container is created, review your sample dataset and follow next steps

addCollectionUtility.shardKeyTooltip: The shard key (field) is used to split your data across many replica sets (shards) to achieve unlimited scalability. It's critical to choose a field that will evenly distribute your data.
addCollectionUtility.partitionKeyTooltip: The {{partitionKeyName}} is used to automatically distribute data across partitions for scalability. Choose a property in your JSON document that has a wide range of values and evenly distributes request volume.
addCollectionUtility.partitionKeyTooltipSqlSuffix: For small read-heavy workloads or write-heavy workloads of any size, id is often a good choice.
addCollectionUtility.uniqueKeysTooltip: Unique keys provide developers with the ability to add a layer of data integrity to their database. By creating a unique key policy when a container is created, you ensure the uniqueness of one or more values per partition key.
addCollectionUtility.analyticalStoreTooltip: Enable analytical store capability to perform near real-time analytics on your operational data, without impacting the performance of transactional workloads.
addCollectionUtility.analyticalStoreDescription: Enable analytical store capability to perform near real-time analytics on your operational data, without impacting the performance of transactional workloads.
addCollectionUtility.vectorPolicyTooltip: Describe any properties in your data that contain vectors, so that they can be made available for similarity queries.

settings.pageOptionsDescription: Choose Custom to specify a fixed amount of query results to show, or choose Unlimited to show as many query results per page.
settings.queryResultsPerPageTooltip: Enter the number of query results that should be shown per page.
settings.entraIdRbacDescription: Choose Automatic to enable Entra ID RBAC automatically. True/False to force enable/disable Entra ID RBAC.
settings.regionSelectionDescription: Changes region the Cosmos Client uses to access account.
settings.selectRegionTooltip: Changes the account endpoint used to perform client operations.
settings.queryTimeoutDescription: When a query reaches a specified time limit, a popup with an option to cancel the query will show unless automatic cancellation has been enabled.
settings.ruLimitDescription: If a query exceeds a configured RU limit, the query will be aborted.
settings.defaultQueryResultsDescription: Select the default view to use when displaying query results.
settings.retrySettingsDescription: Retry policy associated with throttled requests during CosmosDB queries.
settings.maxRetryAttemptsTooltip: Max number of retries to be performed for a request. Default value 9.
settings.fixedRetryIntervalTooltip: Fixed retry interval in milliseconds to wait between each retry ignoring the retryAfter returned as part of the response. Default value is 0 milliseconds.
settings.maxWaitTimeTooltip: Max wait time in seconds to wait for a request while the retries are happening. Default value 30 seconds.
settings.enableContainerPaginationDescription: Load 50 containers at a time. Currently, containers are not pulled in alphanumeric order.
settings.enableCrossPartitionQueryDescription: Send more than one request while executing a query. More than one request is necessary if the query is not scoped to single partition key value.
settings.maxDegreeOfParallelismDescription: Gets or sets the number of concurrent operations run client side during parallel query execution. A positive property value limits the number of concurrent operations to the set value. If it is set to less than 0, the system automatically decides the number of concurrent operations to run.
settings.maxDegreeOfParallelismQuery: Query up to the max degree of parallelism.
settings.priorityLevelDescription: Sets the priority level for data-plane requests from Data Explorer when using Priority-Based Execution. If "None" is selected, Data Explorer will not specify priority level, and the server-side default priority level will be used.
settings.displayGremlinQueryResultsDescription: Select Graph to automatically visualize the query results as a Graph or JSON to display the results as JSON.
settings.enableSampleDatabaseDescription: This is a sample database and collection with synthetic product data you can use to explore using NoSQL queries. This will appear as another database in the Data Explorer UI, and is created by, and maintained by Microsoft at no cost to you.
settings.enableSampleDbAriaLabel: Enable sample db for query exploration
settings.guidRepresentationDescription: GuidRepresentation in MongoDB refers to how Globally Unique Identifiers (GUIDs) are serialized and deserialized when stored in BSON documents. This will apply to all document operations.
settings.ignorePartitionKey: Ignore partition key on document update
settings.ignorePartitionKeyTooltip: If checked, the partition key value will not be used to locate the document during update operations. Only use this if document updates are failing due to an abnormal partition key.
settings.clearHistoryConfirm: Are you sure you want to proceed?
settings.clearHistoryDescription: This action will clear the all customizations for this account in this browser, including:
settings.clearHistoryTabLayout: Reset your customized tab layout, including the splitter positions
settings.clearHistoryTableColumns: Erase your table column preferences, including any custom columns
settings.popupsDisabledError:
We were unable to establish authorization for this account, due to pop-ups being disabled in the browser.
Please enable pop-ups for this site and click on "Login for Entra ID" button
settings.failedToAcquireTokenError: Failed to acquire authorization token automatically. Please click on "Login for Entra ID" button to enable Entra ID RBAC operations

saveQuery.setupCostMessage: For compliance reasons, we save queries in a container in your Azure Cosmos account, in a separate database called “{{databaseName}}”. To proceed, we need to create a container in your account, estimated additional cost is $0.77 daily.
saveQuery.failedToSetupContainerError: Failed to setup a container for saved queries
saveQuery.accountNotSetupError: Failed to save query: account not setup to save queries
saveQuery.name: Name

loadQuery.failedToLoadQueryFromFileError: Failed to load query from file {{fileName}}

executeStoredProcedure.invalidParamConsoleError: Invalid param specified: {{invalidParam}} is not a valid literal value

uploadItems.noFilesSpecifiedError: No files were specified. Please input at least one file.
uploadItems.selectJsonFilesTooltip: Select one or more JSON files to upload. Each file can contain a single JSON document or an array of JSON documents. The combined size of all files in an individual upload operation must be less than 2 MB. You can perform multiple upload operations for larger data sets.

copyNotebook.selectLocation: Select a notebook location to copy
copyNotebook.name: Name

publishNotebook.publishDescription: When published, this notebook will appear in the Azure Cosmos DB notebooks public gallery. Make sure you have removed any sensitive data or output before publishing.
publishNotebook.publishPrompt: Would you like to publish and share "{{name}}" to the gallery?
publishNotebook.name: Name
publishNotebook.outputDoesNotExist: Output does not exist for any of the cells.
publishNotebook.failedToConvertError: Failed to convert {{fileName}} to base64 format

changePartitionKey.failedToStartError: Failed to start data transfer job
changePartitionKey.suboptimalPartitionKeyError: Warning: The system has detected that your collection may be using a suboptimal partition key
changePartitionKey.description: When changing a container’s partition key, you will need to create a destination container with the correct partition key. You may also select an existing destination container.
changePartitionKey.collectionIdTooltip: Unique identifier for the {{collectionName}} and used for id-based routing through REST and all SDKs.
changePartitionKey.partitionKeyWarning: The destination container must not already exist. Data Explorer will create a new destination container for you.

cassandraAddCollection.keyspaceTooltip: Select an existing keyspace or enter a new keyspace id.
cassandraAddCollection.tableIdLabel: Enter CQL command to create the table.
cassandraAddCollection.provisionDedicatedThroughput: Provision dedicated throughput for this table
cassandraAddCollection.provisionDedicatedThroughputTooltip: You can optionally provision dedicated throughput for a table within a keyspace that has throughput provisioned. This dedicated throughput amount will not be shared with other tables in the keyspace and does not count towards the throughput you provisioned for the keyspace. This throughput amount will be billed in addition to the throughput amount you provisioned at the keyspace level.

tables.nullFieldsWarning: Warning: Null fields will not be displayed for editing.
tables.propertyEmptyError: {{property}} cannot be empty. Please input a value for {{property}}
tables.whitespaceError: {{property}} cannot have whitespace. Please input a value for {{property}} without whitespace
tables.propertyTypeEmptyError: Property type cannot be empty. Please select a type from the dropdown for property {{property}}

tableQuerySelect.selectColumns: Select the columns that you want to query.

tableColumnSelection.selectColumns: Select which columns to display in your view of items in your container.

addGlobalSecondaryIndex.projectionQueryTooltip: Learn more about defining global secondary indexes.
addGlobalSecondaryIndex.disabledTitle: A global secondary index is already being created. Please wait for it to complete before creating another one.

stringInput.inputMismatchError: Input {{input}} does not match the selected {{selectedId}}

mongoNotifications.selectTypeWarning: Please select a type for each index.
mongoNotifications.wildcardPathError: Wildcard path is not present in the field name. Use a pattern like

partitionKey.shardKeyTooltip: The shard key (field) is used to split your data across many replica sets (shards) to achieve unlimited scalability. It's critical to choose a field that will evenly distribute your data.
partitionKey.partitionKeyTooltip: is used to automatically distribute data across partitions for scalability. Choose a property in your JSON document that has a wide range of values and evenly distributes request volume.
partitionKey.sqlPartitionKeyTooltipSuffix: For small read-heavy workloads or write-heavy workloads of any size, id is often a good choice.
partitionKey.partitionKeySubtext: For small workloads, the item ID is a suitable choice for the partition key.

throughput.manualToAutoscaleDisclaimer: The starting autoscale max RU/s will be determined by the system, based on the current manual throughput settings and storage of your resource. After autoscale has been enabled, you can change the max RU/s.
throughput.ttlWarningText: The system will automatically delete items based on the TTL value (in seconds) you provide, without needing a delete operation explicitly issued by a client application. For more information see,
throughput.ttlWarningLinkText: Time to Live (TTL) in Azure Cosmos DB
throughput.unsavedEditorWarningPrefix: You have not saved the latest changes made to your
throughput.unsavedEditorWarningSuffix: . Please click save to confirm the changes.
throughput.updateDelayedApplyWarning: You are about to request an increase in throughput beyond the pre-allocated capacity. This operation will take some time to complete.
throughput.scalingUpDelayMessage: Scaling up will take 4-6 hours as it exceeds what Azure Cosmos DB can instantly support currently based on your number of physical partitions. You can increase your throughput to {{instantMaximumThroughput}} instantly or proceed with this value and wait until the scale-up is completed.
throughput.exceedPreAllocatedMessage: Your request to increase throughput exceeds the pre-allocated capacity which may take longer than expected. There are three options you can choose from to proceed:
throughput.instantScaleOption: You can instantly scale up to {{instantMaximumThroughput}} RU/s.
throughput.asyncScaleOption: You can asynchronously scale up to any value under {{maximumThroughput}} RU/s in 4-6 hours.
throughput.quotaMaxOption: Your current quota max is {{maximumThroughput}} RU/s. To go over this limit, you must request a quota increase and the Azure Cosmos DB team will review.
throughput.belowMinimumMessage: You are not able to lower throughput below your current minimum of {{minimum}} RU/s. For more information on this limit, please refer to our service quote documentation.
throughput.saveThroughputWarning: Your bill will be affected as you update your throughput settings. Please review the updated cost estimate below before saving your changes
throughput.applyDelayedMessage: The request to increase the throughput has successfully been submitted. This operation will take 1-3 business days to complete. View the latest status in Notifications.
throughput.applyShortDelayMessage: A request to increase the throughput is currently in progress. This operation will take some time to complete.
throughput.applyLongDelayMessage: A request to increase the throughput is currently in progress. This operation will take 1-3 business days to complete. View the latest status in Notifications.
throughput.throughputCapError: Your account is currently configured with a total throughput limit of {{throughputCap}} RU/s. This update isn't possible because it would increase the total throughput to {{newTotalThroughput}} RU/s. Change total throughput limit in cost management.
throughput.throughputIncrementError: Throughput value must be in increments of 1000

conflictResolution.lwwTooltip: Gets or sets the name of a integer property in your documents which is used for the Last Write Wins (LWW) based conflict resolution scheme. By default, the system uses the system defined timestamp property, _ts to decide the winner for the conflicting versions of the document. Specify your own integer property if you want to override the default timestamp based conflict resolution.
conflictResolution.customTooltip: Gets or sets the name of a stored procedure (aka merge procedure) for resolving the conflicts. You can write application defined logic to determine the winner of the conflicting versions of a document. The stored procedure will get executed transactionally, exactly once, on the server side. If you do not provide a stored procedure, the conflicts will be populated in the
conflictResolution.customTooltipSuffix: . You can update/re-register the stored procedure at any time.

changeFeed.label: Change feed log retention policy
changeFeed.tooltip: Enable change feed log retention policy to retain last 10 minutes of history for items in the container by default. To support this, the request unit (RU) charge for this container will be multiplied by a factor of two for writes. Reads are unaffected.

mongoIndexing.disclaimer: For queries that filter on multiple properties, create multiple single field indexes instead of a compound index.
mongoIndexing.disclaimerSuffix: are only used for sorting query results. If you need to add a compound index, you can create one using the Mongo shell.
mongoIndexing.compoundNotSupported: Collections with compound indexes are not yet supported in the indexing editor. To modify indexing policy for this collection, use the Mongo Shell.
mongoIndexing.aadError: To use the indexing policy editor, please login to the
mongoIndexing.canMakeMoreChangesZero: You can make more indexing changes once the current index transformation is complete.
mongoIndexing.refreshToCheck: Refresh to check if it has completed.
mongoIndexing.canMakeMoreChangesProgress: You can make more indexing changes once the current index transformation has completed. It is {{progress}}% complete.

subSettings.mongoTtlMessage: To enable time-to-live (TTL) for your collection/documents,
subSettings.partitionKeyTooltipTemplate: This {{partitionKeyName}} is used to distribute data across multiple partitions for scalability. The value "{{partitionKeyValue}}" determines how documents are partitioned.

scale.freeTierInfo: With free tier, you will get the first {{ru}} RU/s and {{storage}} GB of storage in this account for free. To keep your account free, keep the total RU/s across all resources in the account to {{ru}} RU/s.
scale.autoScaleCustomSettings: Your account has custom settings that prevents setting throughput at the container level. Please work with your Cosmos DB engineering team point of contact to make changes.
scale.keyspaceSharedThroughput: This table shared throughput is configured at the keyspace

partitionKeyEditor.safeguardWarning: To safeguard the integrity of the data being copied to the new container, ensure that no updates are made to the source container for the entire duration of the partition key change process.
partitionKeyEditor.changeDescription: To change the partition key, a new destination container must be created or an existing destination container selected. Data will then be copied to the destination container.

computedProperties.learnMorePrefix: about how to define computed properties and how to use them.

dataMasking.excludedPathsMustBeArray: excludedPaths must be an array if provided

containerPolicy.createFullTextPolicy: Create new full text search policy

globalSecondaryIndex.indexesDefined: This container has the following indexes defined for it.
globalSecondaryIndex.learnMoreSuffix: about how to define global secondary indexes and how to use them.

throughputInput.autoscaleDescription: Based on usage, your {{resourceType}} throughput will scale from
throughputInput.freeTierWarning: Billing will apply if you provision more than {{ru}} RU/s of manual throughput, or if the resource scales beyond {{ru}} RU/s with autoscale.
throughputInput.fixedStorageNote: When using a collection with fixed storage capacity, you can set up to 10,000 RU/s.

throughputBuckets.label: Throughput Buckets
throughputBuckets.defaultBucketTooltip: The default throughput bucket is used for operations that do not specify a particular bucket.
